All right, we are going into the client tracking spreadsheet. It is awesome and it will keep you super organized with all of your clients. So let's dig into this thing. The first thing that you're going to want to notice are the tabs on the bottom. We have client list. We have client tracking. We have tracking by trainer, so trainer A, B, C, and D. So the first top cell you'll want to notice is the guide or the key for the colors. So gray will be all of your online clients. Green would be large group. Blue would be high ticket or red being the clients that canceled. And instead of deleting them off the sheet, when they're canceled, you want to keep them on there so you can get an idea of your lifetime value and essentially how long it's taking for clients to cancel. And if you see any trends, like maybe everybody's canceling like month four, then month three, you want to do reach outs and extra things like that. But for the most part, we're going to be using these four colors to track all of it. So now we are looking underneath and this is going to, you're going to have your challenge groups separated here because all of these are going to be the people that are moving right to the right hand. You can copy these ones right here and move them right over to your next month once they become EFTs. Or if they don't, you can move them over to challenged. I'm sorry, canceled. So here we have five, six groups, however many challenge groups you have going at once. And you can just name the groups that basically, if they started the first week of January, you can call them January group one, second week of January group two, three, four, right? Just to keep it super simple. And then we have the names in there of the challengers. So then moving down, you'll see that all of these have arrows. So these names are getting pulled directly off of your master client list. So there'll be a group, a list that says challengers and a list that says clients. And you'll put them all in there and you can pick the names. So you are putting the names of any of your clients as soon as they convert over to EFT. So if these people, let's say, so you'll have all of your challengers over here that you can pick from and it'll be different. The names will be different once you get to this. Once you get to this sheet, they'll only be able to pick challengers. And once you get to this sheet, you'll only be able to pick members. Pretty cool. So you'll have all of your members over here and you are tracking their journey to see how long they stay with you and what kind of members they are. So that is all we're doing here. Then you can move to the right where we have the canceled clients. So this is challengers that exited. So when you drop down, you'll be able to find the challenger names. This is for challengers that did not make it over to EFT. So you want to make sure that you are, let's say, Nell here didn't make it over to EFT in this column over to the right. They decided not to continue after the challenge. So we're going to put Nell over here and we are going to turn that red just to show us and we're going to delete it off of here. That's it. So pretty simple. Then all you can do is you can over here once the week is over, change this to January group two and you can copy these names and you can put them up here. And now we just moved. You can put a new title here. But we just moved it over and now they're the next group in the hopper that are about to finish. So that's how you do that and if the challengers continue, you move them to EFT. If they don't continue, you move them over to exited challengers. So we're keeping everything really organized. Then we have exited members. So let's say, you know, Venus here, she cancels. We're going to turn her red over here. Oops, wrong color red. And then we're going to find her over here and we are going to put in Venus. And this is another, the reason that we're tracking it in both places is one is because we want to see how many people canceled throughout the year. Because that way you can export this list and reactivate them. That's the point of tracking it right here is you can send them a new promotion or whatever. But then over on the left hand side, we want to see their journey. So we know that it's now May, whatever, 5th. So Venus canceled 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 months in. That's not bad. So she signed up in January and she canceled 5 months in. That's your baseline for your lifetime value. Hopefully you keep them a little bit longer than that. So now what we're going to do is go to the right. Here is where we have our clients broken up to trainers. So ABCD, four blocks of trainers. And again, you can just toggle down. And right now I have all of the, you know, trainer A has all the online clients. Trainer B has all the high ticket. Trainer C has all the large group. You totally don't have to do it like that. In fact, you like trainer D has a mix of them. You can have a mix of them. However, you want to set it up, but this is just for you to know. Okay. So when, you know, when Tanner here, when he gets moved over and he gets added as an EFT, don't forget you have to assign him to somebody. So I'll put Tanner over here. And now Tanner is has been assigned to trainer B. So then the next thing that we want to do is after we have moved them from Challenger to EFT and then assign their trainer, don't forget to add their trainer to, or I'm sorry, their name to the trainer's tracker, their daily tracker. So let's go into that sheet now. Let's go to trainer B and this is the tracker. So we need to add Tanner because we just, there he is. And you'll have to go into your, your CRM or whatever you have and put his specific information in there. That way you can text them and, you know, and put his address in there so you can send them handwritten cards. So this will be located in like mind body or whatever you're using. There you go. Okay. So now that we are in here, so I just want to back up really quick. That is all that we're doing here. That's all we're doing on this client tracker. You should have all your challengers on this side that you can probably get these groups off of your Challenger spreadsheet. And then you have all your master lists over here of all of your challengers and clients. And then we have, and remember one final thing, don't forget to, when a challenger does continue, don't forget to add them to your master client list here so you know, so you can quickly log in and see how many, how many members you have without having to do all these weird calculations that you show right there. Okay. So we're back into, so that is all that we're doing here, guys. The sheet is done. So let's go back to Trainer B and let's take a look at this thing. So first we have all of the names that Trainer B is assigned to. And then we have all of the phone numbers. And then we have the addresses. So we're using this information to text and send them cards. Then we have a couple columns, last text out and last text back. So this is a manual tracking system where Trainer A can send a text to somebody, whether it's through Scipio, whether it's through their cell phone, whether it's however they want to text people. And then they write down when they text them last. And then when that person texts back, they put the date in there. So just 2011, 2019. That way we know how much we're communicating. So this is one of the horsemen is client communication, right? So if this ever gets farther out than two weeks, then if they're not texting you back, then that's when you need to make an attempt. So as an example, we're going to show you a method on how to automatically send them out a text every two weeks, every week, however frequently you want to. But if they're not texting you back, if you're not getting any responses, then you want to send a purposeful text and then check off attempt one. So let's say Venus has not texted us back. We texted her out at 2.5, but the last time she texted back was 1.1. So it'll automatically update the numbers. So it's like, wow, that's been a long time. It's been a month and a half almost. She hasn't got back to me. So I'm going to do a checkbox of attempt one. And all that means is that you're going to say, hey, is everything okay? It's not your normal text. It's like, hey, is everything okay? I haven't heard from you in a month. I just want to make sure I'm your trainer. You know what I mean? I want to make sure everything's going good. So then you would just put that in the note. Attempted on 2.5. So then that's your last out would be 2.5 and you attempted. Let's say she still doesn't get back to you, then you'll do number two. And you could say, second attempt, 2.18, no response. These are not good at indicators. You know what I mean? So what I do is after I get through four attempts and I try each week with a new attempt to reach out to them, after that fourth one, I start calling them. Because at that point, you're pretty concerned. This is one of your clients and they're just not getting in touch with you at all. So the other thing is, if they say, hey, stop texting me. I don't want to get text or anything like that. Then all you can do is I usually just turn this red and it's just a reminder to not text them. But most people don't say that because you are their coach. So that is how you use this first tracking piece and just make sure that the dates don't get more than two weeks apart because you want 14 day reach outs with them. Next we have handwritten card set. So you should be sending handwritten cards every three months. So this is a reminder and all you do here is you put the date. So you could have 2.1, I will say 2.1.19. Whoops. I'll just delete this last part. Okay. So we sent a handwritten card out on 2.1.19. So we know that by, you know, as soon as May 1st hits, they're going to be due for another card. So you have a new handwritten card and this is the address that you're putting the, that you're addressing it to and you put it in the mail. So that is tracking the second piece or another one of the horsemen of retention. Okay. Then we are going to move farther right and we are going to take a look at visits. So visits here, they are, you're going to want to put the date that you're tracking the visits of your clients. So as an example, if today is the, I don't know, 19th, then that means that you want to take a look at their past, their past week. So this, you should always be tracking visits on a Thursday, a Wednesday, Wednesday or Thursday, whatever you feel more comfortable with. Basically, if they haven't been to the gym by Wednesday or Thursday, then you should be concerned because they're probably not going to come the rest of the week. And as soon as somebody misses one week, they start, you know, rolling and start missing the next week and next thing you know, they're gone. So this is for you to know who to reach out to. So let's say it's Thursday. It's pretty simple. If they had a zero, it's going to turn them red. And if it's anything above a zero, it's going to leave it green or I'm sorry, turn it green. If there's nothing in there, it's just white. So you're going to go through your list of clients for that trainer or your trainer is going to, and they're going to mark off the visits that their clients had. One, zero, two, two, three. Looks like the ones didn't update. We'll get that updated so that those turn green. And that's pretty simple. Now you can shoot a tag previous way in. And if you want to put their stats in there as well, if your coach is really like to always know where their people are at, this is another way that you can track their stats right next to their way in date. So that's how we are using the tracking by trainer. And there's going to be one for each one of these clients, A, B, C, D. Typically you can go up to like 40, 50 clients per trainer. Then you can start adding new accountability coaches or trainers once you get more clients than like 200 or 300 or 400 or something like that. So again, this is how we're using these. It's all pretty simple and you want to divide and conquer and break your clients up amongst your trainers. So the next piece that we are going to go into is cancels. So here we are, cancels, and we have again a dropdown where you can choose the name. And what I like to do is as soon as somebody cancels, I will put, I'll find their name. So let's say it's, you know, whoops. Mercedes. I will put her here. And then I am going to go to the client list and I am going to find Mercedes. Mercedes. There she is. And I'm going to delete her. Once I delete her, I can just reorganize this. And I like to keep it in alphabetical order. That's just me. Whoops. I'll tie these headings up to the top so I don't move. But then it takes out your gaps so you can scroll down and see your active clients. Now we're going to go over to cancels back where we were and we are going to make sure that we have everything filled out for Mercedes. So let's plug her in. Well, she's gone now because I deleted her. She's no longer a member. So that's why you want to do that first. So we'll pick this person. Now these are all red because these are things that we need to confirm. And once they're confirmed, it will turn white. But we need to do this for every single person so nobody slips through the crack. So did they submit a cancel form? If yes, put the date. So 5, 19. Okay. Cancel form was processed. So we received it but was it actually done by somebody at the gym and put into your CRM and you verify that they're not getting billed anymore? So that happened right away for this person. So it was done there. So when is their final payment date? So for me, there's a 30 day notice. So that would be 6, 19 and that is or whatever their final payment date would be for you guys was an exit interview conducted. It should have been. That's one of the other horsemen of retention is exit interview. So yes, it was done. Let's put the link in there. So you should be able to paste the link to your exit interview. You should have a Google drive where you store all of your exit interviews. Now we have sample interviews that you can print off. I've done them on the computer before, but I like printing them off because people like to see that you're filling out notes and all of that kind of stuff. So all I do is I will do the exit interview on a piece of paper. I will literally just take a picture of it with my phone and then I'll upload it right to the Google drive. You know, you can scan it into that looks cleaner, but you can just take a picture of it uploaded right to your Google drive and then pluck that ID right here. So you can always take a look and see why they canceled. And maybe it's a great way to reach back out to them and reactivate them later on. So who did the exit interview? You can just put the initials of the person that conducted it. You can put the main reason why they canceled. So money. And then who processed the cancellation form? So who's the person that did all of this in case you have to look it up in the future? And typically it's like the same person unless you have somebody else do it. So then when it's finally done, when this person is all said and done, they're done at your gym, all the things have been processed, you check this off. And that's it. So that's how you track cancellations. So they don't just get left by the wayside and you make sure that you did the entire process. So that is that sheet. And then the final sheet is attrition. Okay, this sheet is basically tracking how many people you have lost and how many people you have gained and what your net number of clients are. So to make sure that you always know what your net number of clients are, keep in mind. This number here, which is the current month that we're in, in this example, 364 should always match the number of clients that you have on your list here. Right, so when you scroll down and you get down to the bottom, it should say, well, it would say 324. If they're off and somebody's missing, you're going to want to take a closer look at it. Okay, so back over here, all you are doing is entering the people that canceled from that month. So you'll go back to your cancel and you'll say, okay, from this month we have one, two, three, three people that canceled. So you go over here and you put in three. Okay, so when you put in three, it's going to tell you what your attrition is. Okay, so then you're going to want to enter in how many people you signed up. So you go back to your client tracking sheet and it's like, okay, let's just use this march as an example. One, two, three, four, five, six, seven. So we signed up seven people. Let's put that in there. Pretty cool. So we netted four. Four was our net clients, eight year olds in there as well. So I'll update that. But pretty simple and you're just going to want to find the year and the month that you are in and keep this super updated so you know what your attrition is and make sure that your total numbers up here are accurate with how many people are on your list. So that is how this entire spreadsheet works. It's very simple. It's all manual processed, but it'll keep you super organized. Okay, so now that we've kind of explained the tracking sheet that we're going to be using, we can go into the next piece, which will go over the processes that we're using with all of our clients.
